mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate above 4.0% signals monetary tightening that raises discount rates on equity valuations. Higher FEDFUNDS directly compresses present value of future corporate cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer purchasing power. A 3-month increase in UNRATE of more than 0.3 percentage points predicts negative equity returns as earnings expectations decline over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or correlation between 3-month UNRATE change and subsequent 60-day returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly measure consumer demand strength. When both series decline simultaneously over 2 months, aggregate demand weakness signals reduced corporate revenue growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both decline > 2% in 2 months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1187.0K units, RSAFS at 382928M dollars as of 2007-12
failure_condition: Joint decline in HOUST and RSAFS does not predict negative returns with Rank IC > 0.05, or SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 52% of co-declining instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) is an always-on trend signal. Sustained month-over-month growth in INDPRO indicates robust supply-side capacity utilization and productivity, supporting positive corporate earnings and S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO momentum and 5-day forward returns falls below 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window, or positive INDPRO growth does not predict positive returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) and unemployment rate (UNRATE) interact to signal labor market health. When PAYEMS growth slows below 50K/month while UNRATE rises above 5.0%, the combined signal indicates accelerating job losses that trigger negative equity returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 138495K, UNRATE at 5.0% as of 2007-12
regime_hint: labor market weakness with employment deceleration
failure_condition: The interaction signal does not produce negative 60-day returns with Rank IC > 0.04, or SPX returns are positive in more than 48% of instances when both conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL above 2.5% combined with FEDFUNDS above 4.0% signals the Fed is in active tightening mode to combat price pressures. This dual monetary constraint reduces equity valuations and produces negative 20-day forward returns in late-cycle tightening regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 210.565, FEDFUNDS at 4.24% as of 2007-12
failure_condition: Rank IC of the dual condition with 20-day returns is below 0.03, or SPX produces positive returns in more than 55% of instances when both CPIAUCSL > 210 and FEDFUNDS > 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth below 2.0% annualized signals late-cycle or recessionary demand weakness. When quarterly GDPC1 growth decelerates below this threshold, S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days turn negative as earnings expectations contract.
failure_condition: Correlation between GDPC1 growth rate and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05), or SPX produces positive returns in more than 50% of periods with GDPC1 growth < 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) decline of more than 1% month-over-month signals supply-side contraction and reduced capacity utilization. This supply shock predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as profit margins compress and growth expectations fall.
failure_condition: Rank IC between month-over-month INDPRO declines and 5-day forward returns is below 0.02, or SPX produces positive returns in more than 55% of instances following > 1% INDPRO monthly declines.
